defects.defect_text: Gearbox & Drive → Gear bolt loose / fastener loosening
Gearbox & Drive → Gear tooth wear / pitting / scoring
Gearbox & Drive → Gear tooth cracking / fatigue fracture
Gearbox & Drive → Bearing spalling / flaking / failure
Roll Stand → Roll flat spots / localized wear
Roll Stand → Incorrect roll profile / crowning error
Roll Stand → Mandrel fracture or breakage
Roll Stand → Mandrel seizure / stuck mandrel
Roll Stand → Roll bearing failure / overheating
Roll Stand → Guide shoe / guide wear causing tube marks
Roll Stand → Roll slippage / insufficient clamp force
Hydraulics → Pump cavitation (noise, erosion, air bubbles)
Hydraulics → Pump mechanical failure / abnormal noise
Hydraulics → Directional/control valve sticking or sluggish
Hydraulics → Cylinder seal leakage / rod damage
Hydraulics → Relief valve sticking / pressure instability
Cooling & Lube → Lubrication pump failure / insufficient circulation
Cooling & Lube → Filter clogged / restricted flow
Cooling & Lube → Heat exchanger fouling (reduced cooling)
Cooling & Lube → Water ingress / emulsified oil
Electrical → Motor winding short / insulation failure
Electrical → Panel loose terminal / overheating point
Electrical → Encoder/position sensor failure / intermittent signals
Control → PLC communication fault / intermittent I/O
Control → HMI / operator panel freeze or crash
Tooling → Cutter / shear dullness producing burrs or length errors
Product → Wall thickness variation / non-uniform reduction
Product → Longitudinal crack / splitting along length
Product → Surface scoring / scratches / dents / foreign particle marks
Product → Fold / wrinkling / laps (rolled fold defects)
Product → Tube length variation / incorrect cut length
Product → Internal inclusion / non-metallic inclusion causing defects
Product → Seam, lap or overlap (surface discontinuity)
Electrical → Electrical panel or board failure
Roll Stand → Roll fracture or breakage
Feeder → Feed trolley plate requires welding
Roll Stand → Guide support rod bent
Structural → Cover or sheet is loose

root_causes.root_cause_text: Torque → Insufficient torque / incorrect torque spec
Vibration → Component loosening due to vibration/resonance
Assembly → Missing or improper locking device (lock washer/compound)
Lubrication → Insufficient lubricant supply or wrong lubricant
Contamination → Solid debris / abrasive particles in oil or on parts
Lubrication → Loss of lubrication due to pump failure or leak
Alignment → Shaft, roll or mandrel misalignment
Mechanical → Impact or shock overload event
Material → Sub-surface defect or low-quality billet/tube
Contamination → Foreign object ingress (metal chips, grit)
Wear → Mandrel/roll ovalization or dimensional drift
Lubrication → Starvation due to blockage or pump failure
Operation → Excessive loads or incorrect operation parameters
Hydraulics → Suction line air ingestion (loose fittings/cracks)
Hydraulics → Flow restriction (blocked strainer/pipe)
Seal → Seal wear or failure
Cylinder → Rod surface damage / scratch causing seal damage
Hydraulics → Overpressure or pressure spikes
Component → Ageing / fatigue degradation
Pump → Mechanical wear or internal pump failure
Electrical → Motor faults or winding damage
Contamination → Oil dirt/sludge from poor filtration or breakdown
Maintenance → Overdue or poor maintenance practice
Scaling → Heat-exchanger / coolant scaling or deposits
Cooling → Low coolant or lube flow
Electrical → Insulation aging or breakdown
Environment → Moisture ingress / condensation
Electrical → Motor short circuit or earth leakage
Control → Incorrect configuration / parameter on VFD/PLC
Mechanical → Terminal/fastener relaxation due to vibration
Corrosion → Contact oxidation causing high resistance
Wiring → Broken conductor / terminated poorly
Sensor → Sensor surface fouled by oil/dust affecting measurement
Software → PLC/HMI software bug or stored-data corruption
Hardware → PLC/HMI hardware module failure
Rolls → General roll wear from use or abrasive material
Mandrel → Mandrel off-center or misaligned
Process → Excessive strain due to high reduction per pass
Material → Non-metallic inclusion or tramp elements in billet
Setup → Saddle or support incorrectly set during setup
Guide → Guide shoe wear or incorrect geometry
Oil → Oil contaminated with external substances (water/particles)
Clamp → Loose clamping / worn clamp surfaces
Valve → Spool contamination / particulate in valve body
Valve → Valve spool / seat wear
Pipe → Pipe or hose crack leading to leak
Coolant → Leak into lubricant circuits
Environment → Condensation from temperature swings
Electrical → Electromagnetic interference affecting sensors/encoders
Cable → Cable shielding or grounding failure
HMI → HMI software crash or hang
Cutting → Cutter timing misaligned relative to feed
Feeder → Slippage in feed rollers or loss of traction
Process → Foreign particles on tube surface causing marks or damage
Thermal → Rapid temperature changes causing micro-cracks or distortion
Quality → Missing or incomplete incoming material inspection
Wiring → Cable damaged by wear, bending, or mechanical impact
Contamination → Abrasive particles in oil or on surfaces causing wear
Control → Sensor failure due to signal loss, internal fault, or wiring issues
Assembly → Improper alignment during installation/setup
Setup → Incorrect machine setup procedure followed
Logistics → Improper storage of tooling or rolls causing corrosion or distortion
Electrical → Poorly prepared or terminated electrical connections
Sensor → Contamination on sensor surface affecting readings
Human → Tooling change not recorded, causing mismatch in process data
Component → General component failure
Diagnosis → No fault found or not a valid issue
Electrical → Relay trip
Mechanical → Coupling bent
Electrical → MCB trip
Wear → General wear and tear
Mechanical → Weld fatigue or failure
Component → Fan failure
Mechanical → Pump jammed
Electrical → Overload condition
Filtration → Filter clogged
Electrical → Contactor failure
Control → Encoder joint failure
Diagnosis → Root cause not determined
Human → Operator error
Control → Controller failure
Mechanical → Bush broken or damaged
Mechanical → Bearing failure
Electrical → Motor belt failure
Mechanical → Coupling problem
Electrical → Main power is off
Component → Compressor is off
Valve → Solenoid valve failure

corrective_actions.action_text: Torque → Retighten bolts to manufacturer torque spec and log torque values
Fasteners → Apply thread-locker or use prevailing torque nuts / lock washers
Structural → Add damping/isolation mounts, check foundation bolts
Lubrication → Confirm lubricant type, flow rate and refill to spec; log lubrication events
Filtration → Replace oil filters, clean strainers, then sample oil
Alignment → Re-align shaft/gear train using laser/alignment tools
Gearbox → Replace damaged gear to OEM spec and inspect mating components
Bearings → Replace bearings, inspect housing and shaft journals, update lubrication schedule
Lubrication → Perform oil analysis (particle count, water content, viscosity) and act on results
Mandrel → Clean mandrel, or replace if damaged; inspect seating and runout
Mandrel → Inspect and correct guide clearance and guide geometry
Rolls → Regrind roll profile to spec or replace if out of tolerance
Process → Reduce process loads/strain per pass; update operating recipe
Housekeeping → Clean production line, remove foreign particles, improve guards/screens
Clamp → Retighten or replace clamp assemblies and inspect contact surfaces
Hydraulics → Inspect suction hose/piping, shorten suction run, remove restrictions
Hydraulics → Verify fluid level and correct viscosity for ambient temperature
Filtration → Clean or replace suction strainers/filters; add pre-filtration if needed
Hydraulics → Replace or rebuild failing pump, balance and test system pressures
Hydraulics → Service or replace relief valve and calibrate pressure settings
Seals → Replace worn seals, inspect rod surface and housing for damage
Hydraulics → Polish minor rod damage; replace if scoring is severe
Filtration → Replace filters and update filter-change interval; add condition monitoring
Lubrication → Drain and replace contaminated oil; flush system if needed
Cooling → Clean heat exchanger and restore flow rates; check for scale removal measures
Lubrication → Dehydrate oil (centrifuge/degasser) or replace if emulsified
Electrical → Rewind or replace motor; test insulation resistance and thermal sensors
Electrical → Dry and seal motor/enclosure and install moisture control measures
VFD → Reset VFD parameters to OEM recommended; log changes and run test cycles
Electrical → Perform motor insulation and surge tests; diagnose rotor/bar issues
Electrical → Tighten terminations to torque spec and document torque values
Electrical → Clean or replace corroded contact surfaces
Sensors → Clean encoder or replace; verify alignment and shielding
Wiring → Replace damaged cables with correct spec and re-run shielding/grounding
EMI → Improve grounding and cable shielding; reroute power/signal cables separately
Controls → Reload PLC program from verified backup and test I/O mapping
Controls → Replace faulty PLC I/O or communication modules
HMI → Soft reboot HMI; preserve logs for post-mortem
HMI → Replace HMI unit if hardware fault; keep configuration backups
Tooling → Sharpen or replace cutter/shear and verify cut geometry
Cutting → Reset and verify cutter timing and synchronisation with feed
Feeder → Adjust feed rollers, inspect traction surfaces and conveyor alignment
Quality → Reject suspect billet/tube batch; run chemical/micro inspection
Quality → Add ultrasonic or eddy-current in-line inspection for inclusions/cracks
Maintenance → Adjust preventive maintenance schedule based on failure root-cause trends
Reliability → Install vibration and temperature monitoring on bearings and gearbox
Human → Operator training and updated SOPs for setup, shift handover and abnormal-condition response
Filtration → Add offline particle removal (centrifuge/magnet) and improved filtration stages
Filtration → Add pre-filter/strainer to suction line to prevent pump cavitation and wear
Process → Review and limit operating loads; implement alarm thresholds for overload
Cooling → Repair coolant leak paths and ensure separation of coolant and lube loops
Maintenance → Create and enforce PM tasks: bearing checks, roll inspection, filter change logs
Sealing → Inspect gaskets/seal seating and replace with correct spec parts
Filtration → Install additional filtration (offline filter, magnetic separator)
Fasteners → Apply locking compounds or mechanical locks on fasteners
Process → Adjust load settings to within design limits
Hydraulics → Calibrate pressure relief valves and sensors
Sensors → Calibrate or re-zero sensor against known standard
Seals → Inspect and replace worn seals to prevent leaks
Quality → Verify incoming material specifications match requirements
Electrical → Check incoming power supply quality and stability
Cooling → Increase coolant or oil flow to design rates
Guide → Replace worn guide shoes or guide assemblies
Rolls → Implement roll rotation or swapping schedule to balance wear
Filtration → Install pre-filter/strainer in suction line
Monitoring → Install temperature sensors for critical components
Mandrel → Align mandrel using laser or dial gauge tools
Mandrel → Replace worn or damaged mandrel
Lubrication → Conduct oil analysis, then replace if degraded
Lubrication → Dehydrate oil to remove water contamination
Setup → Reset saddle alignment for correct tube support
Sensors → Replace faulty sensor and verify calibration
Controls → Update control software and backup configurations
Setup → Update setup checklist and operator procedures
Hydraulics → Clean contaminated valve spools and housings
Hydraulics → Replace worn or damaged valve assemblies
Wiring → Repair or replace broken/damaged wires
Component → Replace cylinder
Component → Replace component
Structural → Straighten component
Component → Replace pin
Repair → Repair by welding
Action → No corrective action taken
Action → Awaiting spare part
Component → Repair pipe or pipeline
Component → Repair pump
Electrical → Reset relay
Component → Replace pipe or hose
Component → Replace coupling
Component → Replace bolt(s)
Action → Add or adjust packing
Component → Replace shaft
Action → Clean pump
Component → Replace plate
Component → Replace fan
Component → Replace panel or board
Electrical → Reset MCB
Action → Bypass pipe or component
Component → Replace flange
Action → Inspect for leakage
Component → Replace socket
Component → Replace relay
Component → Replace contactor
Action → Diagnosis only, no repair action taken
Action → Operate system manually
Action → Check coil
Process → Clear material jam
Component → Replace chain lock
Electrical → Reset motor trip
Component → Replace carbon brush
Component → Replace cover
Process → Restore flow
Component → Replace bush
Component → Repair encoder
Component → Replace encoder joint
Action → Corrective action not determined
Component → Replace MCB
Action → Adjust fitting
Component → Repair controller
Component → Replace switch
Action → Adjust settings
Repair → Repair threading
Component → Replace bus bar
Repair → Repair by welding, grinding, and machining
Repair → Repair by welding, grinding, and installing new bolts
Action → Remove broken bolt
Component → Replace belt
Action → Issue resolved
Electrical → Switch power on
Component → Replace timer
Component → Machine and install new pin
Component → Machine and install new rod
Component → Replace LMB
Component → Tighten hose
Component → Replace hose
Action → Clean with hot blower
Action → Clean filter

meta.description: Enhanced Pilger Machine ontology — expanded defects, root causes and corrective actions covering mechanical (gearbox, bearings, rolls, mandrel), hydraulics, lubrication/cooling, electrical, controls, tooling and product-quality defects. Validated for internal consistency (codes uniqueness & reference integrity).
meta.validation_notes: Auto-added placeholders for referenced but missing codes; review placeholders.
meta.notes: | Added unique entries from CSV, but CSV defects lack mapped root causes/actions (need SME mapping).

validation.defect_min_links: each defect has 2–5 root causes and 2–5 actions
